segments.text: Then Samuel said to Saul,
The LORD sent me to anoint you king over His people Israel. Now therefore, listen to the words of the LORD.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: This is what the LORD of Hosts says: ‘I witnessed what the Amalekites did to the Israelites when they ambushed them on their way up from Egypt.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: Now go and attack the Amalekites and devote to destruction all that belongs to them. Do not spare them, but put to death men and women, children and infants, oxen and sheep, camels and donkeys.’
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: So Saul summoned the troops and numbered them at Telaim—200,000 foot soldiers and 10,000 men of Judah.
Saul came to the city of Amalek and lay in wait in the valley.
And he warned the Kenites,
Since you showed kindness to all the Israelites when they came up out of Egypt, go on and get away from the Amalekites. Otherwise I will sweep you away with them.
segments.speaker: Saul

segments.text: So the Kenites moved away from the Amalekites.
Then Saul struck down the Amalekites all the way from Havilah to Shur, which is east of Egypt.
He captured Agag king of Amalek alive, but devoted all the others to destruction with the sword.

Saul and his troops spared Agag, along with the best of the sheep and cattle, the fat calves and lambs, and the best of everything else. They were unwilling to destroy them, but they devoted to destruction all that was despised and worthless.
Then the word of the LORD came to Samuel, saying,
I regret that I have made Saul king, for he has turned away from following Me and has not carried out My instructions.
segments.speaker: Samuel was distressed and

segments.text: And Samuel was distressed and cried out to the LORD all that night.
Early in the morning Samuel got up to confront Saul, but he was told,
Saul has gone to Carmel, and behold, he has set up a monument for himself and has turned and gone down to Gilgal.
segments.speaker: Samuel was distressed and

segments.text: When Samuel reached him, Saul said to him,
May the LORD bless you. I have carried out the LORD’s instructions.
segments.speaker: Samuel was distressed and

segments.text: But Samuel replied,
Then what is this bleating of sheep and lowing of cattle that I hear?
segments.speaker: Samuel was distressed and

segments.text: Saul answered,
The troops brought them from the Amalekites; they spared the best sheep and cattle to sacrifice to the LORD your God, but the rest we devoted to destruction.
segments.speaker: Saul

segments.text: Stop!
segments.speaker: Samuel

segments.text: exclaimed Samuel.
Let me tell you what the LORD said to me last night.
segments.speaker: Samuel

segments.text: Tell me,
segments.speaker: Saul

segments.text: Saul replied.
And Samuel said,
Although you were once small in your own eyes, have you not become the head of the tribes of Israel? The LORD anointed you king over Israel
segments.speaker: Saul

segments.text: and sent you on a mission, saying, ‘Go and devote to destruction the sinful Amalekites. Fight against them until you have wiped them out.’
segments.speaker: Saul

segments.text: So why did you not obey the LORD? Why did you rush upon the plunder and do evil in the sight of the LORD?
segments.speaker: Saul

segments.text: But I did obey the LORD,
segments.speaker: Saul

segments.text: Saul replied.
I went on the mission that the LORD gave me. I brought back Agag king of Amalek and devoted the Amalekites to destruction.
segments.speaker: Saul

segments.text: The troops took sheep and cattle from the plunder, the best of the things devoted to destruction, in order to sacrifice them to the LORD your God at Gilgal.
segments.speaker: Saul

segments.text: But Samuel declared:
Does the LORD delight in burnt offerings and sacrificesas much as in obedience to His voice? Behold, obedience is better than sacrifice, and attentiveness is better than the fat of rams.
segments.speaker: Saul

segments.text: For rebellion is like the sin of divination, and arrogance is like the wickedness of idolatry. Because you have rejected the word of the LORD, He has rejected you as king.
segments.speaker: Saul

segments.text: Then Saul said to Samuel,
I have sinned; I have transgressed the LORD’s commandment and your instructions, because I feared the people and obeyed their voice.
segments.speaker: Saul

segments.text: Now therefore, please forgive my sin and return with me so I can worship the LORD.
segments.speaker: Saul

segments.text: I will not return with you,
segments.speaker: Samuel

segments.text: Samuel replied.
For you have rejected the word of the LORD, and He has rejected you as king over Israel.
segments.speaker: Samuel

segments.text: As Samuel turned to go, Saul grabbed the hem of his robe, and it tore.
So Samuel said to him,
The LORD has torn the kingdom of Israel from you today and has given it to your neighbor who is better than you.
segments.speaker: Samuel

segments.text: Moreover, the Glory of Israel does not lie or change His mind, for He is not a man, that He should change His mind.
segments.speaker: Samuel

segments.text: I have sinned,
segments.speaker: Saul

segments.text: Saul replied.
Please honor me now before the elders of my people and before Israel. Come back with me, so that I may worship the LORD your God.
segments.speaker: Saul

segments.text: So Samuel went back with Saul, and Saul worshiped the LORD.
Then Samuel said,
Bring me Agag king of the Amalekites.
segments.speaker: Saul

segments.text: Agag came to him cheerfully, for he thought,
Surely the bitterness of death is past.
segments.speaker: Saul

segments.text: But Samuel declared:
As your sword has made women childless, so your mother will be childless among women.
segments.speaker: Saul

segments.text: And Samuel hacked Agag to pieces before the LORD at Gilgal.
Then Samuel went to Ramah, but Saul went up to his home in Gibeah of Saul.
And to the day of his death, Samuel never again visited Saul. Samuel mourned for Saul, and the LORD regretted that He had made Saul king over Israel.
